narrative.headline: 2019 Honda HR-V: electrical glitches and stalling lead owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 211 complaints with NHTSA. The biggest theme is the electrical system, named in 29% of reports, often as warning lights coming on. The next cluster is fuel and propulsion: fuel-system and gasoline issues each appear in 17% of reports and fuel/propulsion in 13%, frequently describing the car stalling or losing power while driving. About 12% flag the forward-collision-avoidance system. Reports are mostly non-injury, but a few involve a crash, so take any sudden warning light, stall, or power loss seriously.

issues.sample: SINCE THE PURCHASE OF THE VEHICLE, THREE INDICATOR LIGHTS HAVE GONE ON AND OFF APPROXIMATELY 20 TIMES AND THE VEHICLE IS IN THE DEALER'S SERVICE DEPARTMENT NOW FOR THE THIRD TIME TO TRY AND REMEDY THE ISSUE. THE INDICATOR LIGHTS ARE: THE YELLOW CHECK ENGINE LIGHT, THE YELLOW ELEC
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most-reported area, in 61 complaints (29%), commonly warning lights flicking on. One report involved a crash. Don't ignore dashboard warning lights.

issues.sample: TL* THE CONTACT OWNS A 2019 HONDA HR-V. THE CONTACT STATED THAT WHILE DRIVING AT LOW SPEEDS, THE VEHICLE STALLED WITHOUT WARNING. THE CONTACT RECEIVED NOTIFICATION OF NHTSA CAMPAIGN NUMBER: 20V314000 (FUEL SYSTEM, GASOLINE). THE CONTACT STATED THE MANUFACTURER EXCEEDED A REASONAB
issues.title: Fuel System
issues.blurb: 35 complaints (17%), with owners describing the car stalling without warning. No crashes, fires, or injuries reported here.

issues.title: Gasoline
issues.blurb: Tagged in 35 complaints (17%), overlapping with the fuel-system stalling reports. No crashes, fires, or injuries reported.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 28 complaints (13%) about losing acceleration or power, sometimes at highway speed. No crashes, fires, or injuries reported.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 26 complaints (12%) about the driver-assist system, including the car stopping unexpectedly. One report involved a crash.
